name: Embalse de Portaje Loop
name_original: Torrejoncillo — circular por la Presa del Embalse de Portaje
description: Explore the easy 16.6-mile mountain biking route around Portaje Reservoir in Torrejoncillo, enjoying dehesa landscapes and birdwatching.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Torrejoncillo are situated within the Sierra de Gata region of Extremadura, Spain. The landscape is characterized by extensive forests, unique rock formations, and a network of natural paths. This area features mountains and valleys densely covered with chestnut, oak, and pine trees, alongside numerous streams and natural pools. The varied terrain includes forest tracks and technical sections, catering to different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Torrejoncillo The most popular mountain bike trail is Calzadilla Loop from Coria, a 29.2 miles (46.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 49 minutes to complete. This route explores the varied landscapes near Coria, showcasing the region's natural beauty. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is La Encamisá of Torrejoncillo – Plaza Mayor of Torrejoncillo loop from Torrejoncillo, a moderate 35.4 miles (57.0 km) path. This route offers a comprehensive exploration of the local area, passing through diverse natural settings. Local mountain bikers also love the Embalse de Portaje Loop, a 16.6 miles (26.7 km) trail leading through areas around the Embalse de Portaje, often completed in about 1 hour 57 minutes. Mountain biking around Torrejoncillo is defined by dense woodlands, varied rock formations, and scenic valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easier paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Torrejoncillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Torrejoncillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails are available around Torrejoncillo?
A: The area offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails, from easier paths to more challenging routes. You'll find trails through extensive forests, past unique rock formations, and along natural paths. The terrain includes forest tracks and technical sections, catering to various skill levels.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Torrejoncillo?
A: Yes, there are options for less experienced riders. Out of the 36 available mountain bike tours, 2 are classified as easy. These routes are generally flatter and shorter, providing a good introduction to mountain biking in the region. An example of an easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1252193495/embalse-de-portaje-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Portaje Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 16.6 miles long.

Q: What are the more challenging mountain bike routes near Torrejoncillo?
A: For experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, there are 7 difficult routes available. These trails often feature significant elevation gains and technical sections. An example of a difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022484934/calzadilla-loop-from-coria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calzadilla Loop from Coria&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 29.2 miles with over 500 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I expect to see on the trails around Torrejoncillo?
A: The Sierra de Gata region, where Torrejoncillo is located, is rich in natural beauty. You'll encounter dense forests of chestnut, oak, and pine trees, numerous streams, impressive rock formations, and crystal-clear natural pools. While riding, you might also spot local wildlife like deer and birds of prey. For specific landmarks, consider routes that pass by historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6053196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Portezuelo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical points of interest along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate historical landmarks. For instance, the region features medieval bridges like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6047850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Bridge of Coria&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron Bridge of Coria&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6053196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Portezuelo&lt;/a&gt; is a notable castle that can be reached via some trails, offering a glimpse into the area's past.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Torrejoncillo?
A: The mountain bike trails around Torrejoncillo are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 5.0 stars from 14 reviews. Over 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the scenic beauty and the well-maintained network of paths.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Torrejoncillo?
A: The Extremadura region generally offers pleasant conditions for outdoor activities for much of the year. Spring and autumn are typically ideal, with milder temperatures and lush landscapes. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended, while winters are generally mild but can have occasional rain.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Torrejoncillo are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022484934/calzadilla-loop-from-coria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calzadilla Loop from Coria&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1252193495/embalse-de-portaje-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Portaje Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offer varied distances and difficulty levels.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails?
A: While many natural areas in Spain are dog-friendly, it's always best to check local regulations for specific trails or protected areas. Generally, dogs are welcome on trails if kept under control, especially in rural and forested areas. Be mindful of wildlife and other trail users, and ensure your dog is accustomed to longer rides and varied terrain.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trails?
A: Torrejoncillo and nearby towns like Coria offer various parking options. For specific trailheads, parking availability can vary, but generally, you can find parking in or near the starting points of the routes, especially in the towns that serve as access points to the Sierra de Gata Mountain Bike Centre trails.

Q: Are there any permits required for mountain biking in Torrejoncillo?
A: Mountain biking in the Sierra de Gata region, including around Torrejoncillo, generally does not require specific permits for recreational use on established trails. However, it's always advisable to respect local signage, stay on marked paths, and be aware of any specific regulations that might apply to certain protected natural areas.
tips: Torrejoncillo is a small and charming typical Extremadura town with a nice square, small narrow streets and beautiful festivals.
A small Hermitage of the Virgen del Casar on the banks of the Portaje Reservoir near the town of Torrejoncillo.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is classified as easy and is described as not very physically or technically demanding. It's a great option for those new to mountain biking or looking for a relaxed ride.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The route offers wonderful natural landscapes, primarily traversing traditional dehesas with cork and holm oaks. The central feature is the Embalse de Portaje (Portaje Reservoir), a peaceful natural enclave. You'll also pass by the settlement of La Encamisá of Torrejoncillo and the Ermita de la Virgen del Casar.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of paths through the dehesas. While generally accessible and easy, be aware that depending on weather conditions, you might encounter "callejas pringonas" (muddy lanes), which can add a varied element to your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the Mediterranean climate of Extremadura, spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for mountain biking. The weather is milder, and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring cooler, potentially wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, specific rules for dogs on this particular route are not detailed. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock in the dehesas, and to always clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to ride this mountain bike route around the Embalse de Portaje. It's freely accessible for public enjoyment.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts near Torrejoncillo. While specific parking areas are not detailed, you can typically find parking in or around the town of Torrejoncillo, or at accessible points near the Embalse de Portaje where the route begins.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families?
faqs.answer: Yes, due to its easy difficulty rating and scenic, non-technical terrain, this mountain bike route is well-suited for families looking for a pleasant outdoor activity together. The circular nature also makes it convenient.

faqs.question: Can I expect to see wildlife, especially birds, along the route?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The Portaje Reservoir is a recognized important wetland and a haven for birdwatching, hosting over two hundred species. You might spot spoonbills, teal, mallards, herons, white storks, and even Imperial Eagles, making it an excellent spot for nature observation.

faqs.question: Are there any services like cafes or accommodation nearby?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends near Torrejoncillo, a town where you can find local services such as cafes, restaurants, and potentially accommodation options to refuel or stay overnight before or after your ride.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, as indicated by its name, "circular por la Presa del Embalse de Portaje," this is a circular route, meaning you will end up back at your starting point without having to retrace your steps.

waypoints.name: Ermita de la Virgen del Casar
